feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: The code is well-structured and meets all basic requirements, but is missing the final 25% polish in most areas. The main gaps are in advanced confidence scoring, error recovery strategies, and performance metrics that would elevate it from good to exceptional.

DO NOT CHANGE (these sections are passing):
  ✓ code_api_design: scored 100% - keep function signature, docstring, and input flexibility unchanged
  ✓ code_idiomaticness: scored 100% - maintain current use of csv module and dataclass structure

REQUIRED FIXES (ordered by point impact):

  FIX 1: code_robustness.error_handling (current: 75%, 1.2 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% of full score despite structured error handling
    Expected: Perfect error handling with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Good try/except blocks with specific exceptions and meaningful warnings
    → ACTION: Add error recovery strategies - when csv.reader fails with one quote mode, automatically try alternative quote modes and record which one succeeded, then add timeout handling for very large files.

  FIX 2: code_correctness.edge_case_resilience (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% of full score despite handling all specified edge cases
    Expected: Perfect edge case handling with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Handles empty rows, mixed quoting, jagged rows, and trailing delimiters well
    → ACTION: Add handling for CSV files with BOM (Byte Order Mark) by detecting and stripping UTF-8/UTF-16 BOMs at the start of content, and add detection of escaped quotes within quoted fields.

  FIX 3: code_correctness.delimiter_detection (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% of full score despite meeting all basic requirements
    Expected: Perfect delimiter detection with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Good implementation with csv.Sniffer and fallback, but room for improvement
    → ACTION: Add confidence scoring to delimiter detection - return a confidence percentage based on consistency of field counts across sample rows, and prefer delimiters with higher confidence scores when multiple candidates exist.

  FIX 4: code_correctness.header_handling (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% of full score despite meeting all basic requirements
    Expected: Perfect header detection with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Sophisticated header detection with 6 checks, but could be enhanced
    → ACTION: Add machine learning-style scoring to header detection - weight the 6 existing checks and return a confidence score (0-1) for header presence, using this score to make more nuanced decisions in borderline cases.

  FIX 5: code_robustness.data_integrity (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% of full score despite comprehensive anomaly reporting
    Expected: Perfect data integrity with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Reports all data modifications and preserves structure information
    → ACTION: Add data validation checksums - compute and return a hash of the original raw content and a hash of the cleaned data, allowing users to verify data integrity and detect any unexpected transformations.

  FIX 6: code_robustness.return_metadata (current: 75%, 0.8 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% of full score despite structured ParseResult
    Expected: Perfect metadata with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Rich metadata including performance warnings and Unicode detection
    → ACTION: Add parsing performance metrics to ParseResult - include fields for parse_time_seconds, memory_peak_mb, and rows_per_second to help users optimize their CSV processing workflows.

  FIX 7: code_testability.pure_function (current: 50%, 0.7 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Only 50% score despite being deterministic and side-effect free
    Expected: Perfect purity with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Function is pure but may have minor implementation details affecting score
    → ACTION: Ensure the function never modifies the input source object's state - if source is a file object, save and restore its position explicitly, and add a parameter to control whether file position should be preserved.

  FIX 8: code_testability.modularity (current: 75%, 0.5 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% despite having 8+ helper functions
    Expected: Perfect modularity with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Exceptionally well-decomposed with focused helper functions
    → ACTION: Extract the delimiter scoring logic into a separate DelimiterScorer class with methods for each scoring criterion (consistency_score, quote_penalty_score, field_count_variance), making the scoring algorithm more testable and extensible.

  FIX 9: code_testability.test_examples (current: 75%, 0.5 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing 25% despite comprehensive docstring examples
    Expected: Perfect test examples with advanced domain expertise
    Found: Three detailed examples covering different CSV formats and edge cases
    → ACTION: Add a fourth example demonstrating error recovery - show a malformed CSV with mixed delimiters and how the function handles it, including the specific warnings generated and the fallback behavior used.
